entries.title: Drowned Harbor Tale Preview
entries.entry_condition: The host highlights the design-only Drowned Harbor Tale preview in a future authorized Tale-selection flow.
entries.exit_condition: The host backs out or confirms a future authorized session setup path; P0.10 itself performs neither action.
entries.purpose: Present the Tale identity, intended seat range, content profile, public premise, accessibility availability, and design-only status without implying production availability.
visual_guidance: Use a clean Low-Tide key-art crop with lighthouse authority and readable landmark silhouettes.
Keep the design-only label and seat range on a plain high-contrast surface rather than distressed art.
Use scarce Lantern Amber only for the current focus and active accessibility affordance.
negative_constraints: No Play or Start action in this design-only package.
No storefront, release-date, legal-name, production-readiness, or final-rating claim.
No muddy sepia treatment that collapses the board and text into one value range.
human_validation_questions: Can viewers distinguish Tale premise, design-only status, and accessibility options from living-room distance?
Does the preview feel original and atmospheric without implying that Drowned Harbor is playable?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no Tale-selection implementation, final key art, legal title, rating, store presentation, or production availability is approved.

entries.title: Local Stable-Seat Lobby
entries.entry_condition: A future authorized local session reaches seat setup before any private role, faction, objective, or inventory assignment.
entries.exit_condition: The host backs out or all required public seat and mode settings are confirmed for an authorized future session.
entries.purpose: Show 1–8 stable seats, connected controllers, public seat ownership, mode compatibility, fallback behavior, and accessibility entry points without exposing future private assignments.
required_information: Cooperative fallback when a mode is unsupported
Local play requires no account or phone
visual_guidance: Use a stable eight-position seat grid that collapses gracefully for fewer seats without changing identity order.
Supplement controller color with seat number, controller glyph, and text state.
Keep mode incompatibility and fallback explanation adjacent to the affected selection.
negative_constraints: No hidden role, faction, objective, inventory, infection, or future form preview.
No requirement for account, internet, phone, invitation, join code, or remote approval in local play.
No character reroll or fresh-seat implication when reusing a stable seat.
human_validation_questions: Can eight local players identify their seat and controller without relying on color alone?
Are unsupported-mode fallbacks understandable without making cooperative play feel like an error state?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no lobby scene, controller mapping, mode implementation, account flow, or seat-assignment algorithm is approved.

entries.title: Low-Tide Arrival Board
entries.entry_condition: The public Tale state enters Low-Tide Arrival and all seat-private introductions have cleared their authorized surfaces.
entries.exit_condition: The public record threshold commits the transition to Bellhouse Ledger or the session leaves the Tale.
entries.purpose: Establish causeway, landmarks, open routes, distant returning waterline, seat positions, first public objective, captions, and action ownership at television distance.
required_information: Stable seat positions and current actor
visual_guidance: Use elevated three-quarter board framing with lighthouse as dominant vertical anchor.
Separate causeway, Bellhouse, Salt Market, lifeboat shed, and lighthouse through silhouette and value rather than labels alone.
Render tide evidence through route edges, reflections, waterline, debris, and markers rather than a single color overlay.
negative_constraints: No uncontrolled fog, scenery, caption, or UI panel may hide an essential route or seat.
No essential route state may depend on blue versus green alone.
No continuous low-value brown or gray wash that makes timber, mud, slate, water, canvas, and brass indistinguishable.
human_validation_questions: Can viewers identify landmarks, seats, open routes, and returning water from representative television distance?
Does the cool-dark atmosphere preserve material and local-color separation instead of becoming muddy?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no board camera, route renderer, seat rail, objective UI, animation, or readability claim is approved.

entries.title: Bellhouse Ledger Decision
entries.entry_condition: The Bellhouse Ledger presents an authoritative public record, unresolved position, or legal public priority decision.
entries.exit_condition: The active authority cancels a reversible preview or confirms one displayed legal priority and the result commits once.
entries.purpose: Present Ledger evidence, public counts, unresolved positions, legal priorities, consequence previews, and confirmation without implying prophecy or hidden allegiance.
visual_guidance: Use swollen paper, readable index marks, oxidized brass, vertical ropes, and one clear bell silhouette.
Separate record evidence from selectable priorities through shape, spacing, and focus treatment.
Keep public count and unresolved position persistent when browsing consequences.
negative_constraints: No hidden-seat highlight, traitor hint, private faction motif, or correct-answer glow.
No cinematic bell effect may cover the public count or legal priorities.
No selection may commit on first focus movement without a separate confirm action.
human_validation_questions: Can players distinguish record evidence, unresolved positions, and legal priorities without assuming one is prophecy?
Is the confirmation boundary clear under local group discussion and interrupted narration?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no Ledger mechanics, final copy, controller behavior, animation, timing, or privacy claim is approved.

entries.title: Lighthouse Council Direction Choice
entries.entry_condition: The public Tale reaches Lighthouse Council with a validated set of currently legal direction options.
entries.exit_condition: The group cancels a preview or confirms one public option and the deterministic High Water transition becomes pending.
entries.purpose: Support group discussion of several plausible lighthouse directions, showing public consequences and confirmation without moralizing or foreshadowing hidden outcomes.
required_information: Current public truth and mechanism state
Public consequence preview for each direction
No public timer unless separately active
visual_guidance: Use lighthouse lens and directional wedge motifs without turning one option into a brighter correct path.
Allow the board and relevant route geography to remain visible behind or beside the options.
Use silence and reduced panel density during discussion-heavy moments.
negative_constraints: No good, bad, heroic, evil, recommended, or correct-answer styling unless an authoritative public rule states it.
No hidden Bellmarked, Tidebound, bargain, or ending state may alter the shared options or music before reveal.
No forced countdown or urgency indicator when no public timer exists.
human_validation_questions: Do all legal directions feel plausible enough to support genuine group discussion?
Can players review consequences and cancel without losing board context or mistaking preview for commitment?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no Council option set, final consequence, voting rule, timer, controller flow, or implementation is approved.

entries.title: Public Harbor Bargain Offer
entries.entry_condition: A governed Harbor bargain becomes available and its authorized public terms can be shown without exposing private cost or target data.
entries.exit_condition: The offer is refused, deferred where legal, or the active seat proceeds to the authorized private terms surface.
entries.purpose: State that an optional bargain exists, identify public terms, and present accept-for-review or refuse actions without sales pressure or acceptance implication.
visual_guidance: Use water, cloudy glass, brass, and record motifs to communicate a precise transaction rather than a generic evil offer.
Keep Proceed to Private Review and Refuse equal in visual weight.
State explicitly that no bargain has committed on this screen.
negative_constraints: No private cost, hidden objective, target, infection, role, or faction information on the shared screen.
No demonic, cash-register, limited-time, reward-glow, or punishment-threat sales language.
No first selection may count as acceptance.
human_validation_questions: Do players understand that proceeding opens private review rather than accepting the bargain?
Are refusal and private review equally legible without social or visual coercion?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no bargain terms, private-device flow, acceptance rule, consequence, or runtime implementation is approved.

entries.title: Private Harbor Bargain Terms
entries.entry_condition: The active seat legally requests private bargain review at a safe handoff and the shared-screen neutral shield has committed.
entries.exit_condition: The seat refuses or confirms the exact private terms, acknowledges the result, and the public shield is restored before shared play resumes.
entries.purpose: Present exact private benefit, cost, affected seat state, and confirmation on the authorized private surface while the shared screen reveals nothing beyond private review in progress.
visual_guidance: Use a neutral shared shield with no icon, color, sound, or wording that hints at the private terms.
On the authorized private surface, separate benefit, cost, affected state, and irreversible confirmation.
Clear the private presentation immediately on interruption before restoring the neutral shield.
negative_constraints: No private content in shared captions, public transcript, public audio, mirrored display, or other seat summaries.
No auto-accept, timeout acceptance, default-confirm focus, or hidden cost.
No authority transfer while the private surface is exposed.
human_validation_questions: Does the neutral shared shield avoid leaking the existence, type, desirability, or target of private terms?
Can the active player review and refuse exact terms without accidentally committing or exposing the surface?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no private-surface technology, bargain terms, timeout, mirroring behavior, or security claim is approved.

entries.title: High Water Commitment and Transition
entries.entry_condition: A Lighthouse Council direction has committed and authoritative High Water transformation state is ready to apply once.
entries.exit_condition: The transformation summary, changed route categories, affected public mechanisms, and current seat positions are visible and acknowledged.
entries.purpose: Separate the committed before-state, optional cinematic transition, and authoritative after-state so High Water is understood as a deterministic board and rule transformation rather than a visual overlay.
required_information: No player input changes the committed result
visual_guidance: Use advancing water, route distortion, moving debris, altered silhouettes, and lighthouse response while preserving seat and landmark readability.
Show changed categories as plain persistent text before returning control.
Skipping animation must arrive at the same after-state and summary.
negative_constraints: No gameplay choice, random result, or authority transfer during the transformation presentation.
No route, objective, form, resource, or ending change may exist only in animation or audio.
No screen-filling wave, fog, bloom, shake, or darkness that prevents understanding the committed result.
human_validation_questions: Do players understand which public state categories changed before control returns?
Does skipping the presentation preserve comprehension and emotional weight without altering the result?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no High Water runtime mutation, cinematic, timing, camera, input lock, or human comprehension claim is approved.

entries.title: High Water Transformed Board
entries.entry_condition: The deterministic High Water transformation has committed, its summary has been acknowledged, and no private surface is exposed.
entries.exit_condition: The Tale advances to Last Light, ends, pauses, or enters another authorized public or private decision surface.
entries.purpose: Present the transformed board with land, water, blocked, damaged, and recovery routes; current seat locations; active rescue crises; public forms; and newly legal actions.
required_information: High Water stage and tide state
visual_guidance: Use clear water-only connectors, partially submerged landmarks, rope and boat affordances, and altered silhouettes.
Retain recognizable Low-Tide geography so transformation reads as the same board under changed authority.
Keep dark and stormy color distinct through cool water, slate, timber, canvas, brass, clothing, and scarce amber.
negative_constraints: No generic full-screen blue overlay or muddy black-brown grade may substitute for transformed geometry and routes.
No transformed or Restless seat may disappear from the seat rail or active-action sequence.
No hidden faction, private objective, latent form, or private route knowledge may appear publicly.
human_validation_questions: Can players map Low-Tide geography to the transformed High Water board and identify changed route types?
Are rescue windows, hazards, forms, seat positions, and current authority legible without color or audio alone?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no transformed board authority, route renderer, hazard timing, movement rule, or living-room readability claim is approved.

entries.title: Tidebound Public Transformation
entries.entry_condition: A stable seat's Tidebound transformation has authoritatively committed and may now be publicly revealed.
entries.exit_condition: The same stable seat, new public form, displayed capabilities and restrictions, and current legal actions are acknowledged.
entries.purpose: Reveal a Tidebound form as an active continuation of the same stable seat, preserving identity, agency, history, position, and privacy while showing public rule changes.
visual_guidance: Retain recognizable evidence of the original character through seat marker, silhouette anchor, carried object, or posture.
Use curved water-pulled contours and layered memory shapes without generic zombie anatomy.
Update the existing seat tile rather than replacing it with a new identity.
negative_constraints: No player-eliminated, dead-player, monster-player, zombie, possession, or replacement language.
No automatic faction, objective, betrayal, cure, spread, or ending implication beyond displayed public rules.
No loss of turn ownership or seat rail presence merely because the form changed.
human_validation_questions: Do players recognize the transformed figure as the same stable seat rather than a replacement or enemy NPC?
Are the new public capabilities and restrictions understandable without revealing private allegiance or objectives?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no Tidebound trigger, conversion rule, visual asset, ability set, privacy behavior, or runtime transformation is approved.

entries.title: Last Light Final Decision
entries.entry_condition: The Tale enters Last Light with a validated set of currently legal final options and public consequence summaries.
entries.exit_condition: The group cancels a preview or confirms one legal final option and authoritative ending calculation begins once.
entries.purpose: Present final options, public consequences, unresolved private-attribution note, and irreversible confirmation without labeling one route as universally good or bad.
required_information: Private attribution follows on authorized surfaces
visual_guidance: Reduce density and preserve board, lighthouse, Bellhouse, lifeboat, and Harbor mechanism context behind the final options.
Use separate material and mechanism motifs for each option without universal victory or defeat color coding.
Allow silence, plain-system text, transcript, and consequence review throughout discussion.
negative_constraints: No good-ending, bad-ending, heroic, cowardly, true-ending, recommended, or correct-choice labels.
No auto-selection, majority assumption, hidden countdown, or first-focus commitment.
No private role, faction, objective, bargain, or ending-carrier result on the shared option screen.
human_validation_questions: Can players compare final options and consequences without being visually pushed toward one moral verdict?
Is irreversible confirmation unmistakable while still allowing group discussion, replay, and transcript access?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no final option eligibility, voting, consequence, ending calculation, controller rule, or runtime implementation is approved.

entries.title: Mixed Public Outcome Attribution
entries.entry_condition: Authoritative ending calculation has completed and public seat, form, faction, and Harbor results are available for separate attribution.
entries.exit_condition: All public segments are acknowledged and any controlled-private results have been delivered on authorized surfaces before session closure or future Chronicle handling.
entries.purpose: Present mixed outcomes as separate seat, faction, form, and Harbor results without flattening them into one universal victory, defeat, score, or moral verdict.
required_information: Public result for each stable seat
visual_guidance: Use separate columns or cards for stable-seat, faction, and Harbor results with stable seat ordering preserved.
Allow incompatible visual motifs to coexist rather than resolving into one victory color or banner.
Keep public result text plain, persistent, and available after voice or music ends.
negative_constraints: No universal Victory, Defeat, Winner, Loser, score, star rating, or moral grade over mixed outcomes.
No private objective, bargain term, unrevealed faction detail, or controlled-private result in public history.
No seat result may be omitted because the seat transformed, became Restless, disconnected, or changed controller.
human_validation_questions: Can every player identify the public result for their stable seat, form, faction, and the Harbor?
Does the layout preserve mixed or incompatible outcomes without implying one universal winner or moral verdict?
entries.approval_boundary: Storyboard only; no final ending text, attribution algorithm, Chronicle integration, private delivery, credits flow, or runtime UI is approved.
